summary: Matthew Cox discusses his history as a conman, detailing his methods of mortgage fraud, identity theft, and other scams. He reflects on the ethics of his actions and the motivations behind them, as well as the consequences he faced.

main_entities.name: Mortgage Fraud
main_entities.context: Various methods used to defraud banks during mortgage applications, including falsifying documents and manipulating appraisals.

main_entities.name: Synthetic Identities
main_entities.context: Creation of fake identities using stolen or fabricated information to obtain credit and loans.

main_entities.name: Eagle Lending
main_entities.context: The mortgage company where Matthew Cox began his career and committed his first fraudulent actions.

main_entities.name: Bank of Ybor
main_entities.context: A fake bank created by Matthew Cox to provide fraudulent bank statements.

main_entities.name: Omerta
main_entities.context: The code of silence among criminals, and Matthew Cox's evolving perspective on loyalty and cooperation.

main_entities.name: Ybor City
main_entities.context: Area in Tampa where Matthew Cox ran a property flipping scam using synthetic identities.

main_entities.name: Gretchen Zaas
main_entities.context: Matthew Cox's manager at Eagle Lending who encouraged his initial fraudulent activities.

main_entities.name: Scott Kugno
main_entities.context: Individual whose identity Matthew Cox stole after leaving Tampa.

main_entities.name: Rebecca Houck (Becky)
main_entities.context: Matthew Cox's girlfriend who accompanied him on the run and was involved in some of his scams.

main_entities.name: Straw Man Scam
main_entities.context: Using a real person with good credit to obtain loans with no intention of repayment.

main_entities.name: Hard Money Lenders
main_entities.context: Lenders who provide short-term loans secured by real estate, often with higher interest rates and fees.

main_entities.name: Federal Probation
main_entities.context: The supervised release Matthew Cox was under when he began his larger scams.

main_entities.name: Washington Mutual
main_entities.context: Bank that was defrauded by Matthew Cox, leading to a close call with law enforcement.

key_details.fact: Matthew Cox created synthetic identities by using children's Social Security numbers and fake birth certificates.
Matthew Cox used the names of characters from the movie Reservoir Dogs for his synthetic identities.
Matthew Cox was able to get away with fraud because people naturally seemed to trust him.
Matthew Cox was on federal probation when he started running a larger scam involving flipping properties.
Matthew Cox was caught by Washington Mutual, but he talked them into allowing him to refinance the properties.
Matthew Cox got plastic surgery to change his appearance.
Matthew Cox satisfied the loans on Michael Shanahan's house and then refinanced the house.
Matthew Cox used homeless people's identities to get real driver's licenses.
